items.title: RBI imposes monetary penalty on The Jalore Central Co-operative Bank Limited, Rajasthan
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has, by an order dated May 22, 2026, imposed a monetary penalty of ₹2 lakh (Rupees Two Lakh only) on The Jalore Central Co-operative Bank Limited, Rajasthan (the bank) for non-compliance with certain directions issued by RBI on ‘Know Your Customer (KYC)’. This penalty has been imposed in exercise of powers conferred on RBI under the provisions of Section 47A(1)(c) read with Sections 46(4)(i) and 56 of the Banking Regulation Act. The statutory inspection of the bank was conducted by National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (NABARD) with reference to it

items.title: RBI imposes monetary penalty on Shree Laxmi Co-operative Bank Ltd., Pune
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has, by an order dated May 22, 2026, imposed a monetary penalty of ₹2 lakh (Rupees Two Lakh only) on Shree Laxmi Co-operative Bank Ltd., Pune (the bank) for non-compliance with certain directions issued by RBI on ‘Know Your Customer (KYC)’; `Maintenance of Deposit Accounts – Primary (Urban) Co-operative Banks’ and `Inoperative Accounts / Unclaimed Deposits in Banks – Revised Instructions’. This penalty has been imposed in exercise of powers conferred on RBI under the provisions of Section 47A(1)(c) read with Sections 46(4)(i) and 56 of the Banking Regulation Act

Mahisagar, Gujarat (the bank), for non-compliance with certain directions issued by RBI on ‘Loans and advances to directors, their relatives, and firms / concerns in which they are interested’. This penalty has been imposed in exercise of powers conferred on RBI under the provisions of Section 47A(1)(c) read with Sections 46(4)(i) and 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949. items.title: Middle East tensions rattle Asia markets after fresh U.S. strikes in Iran
items.summary: Asia-Pacific markets fell Thursday while oil prices surged after fresh U.S. strikes in Iran heightened fears of a wider Middle East conflict.

items.title: NorthWind Estates buys 5acre land in Yamuna Expressway region for Rs 189 cr via auction
items.summary: The company in a statement said that it has emerged as the highest bidder in a recent auction conducted by the Yamuna Expressway Industrial Development Authority (YEIDA).

items.title: Kuwait air defenses activated against 'missile and drone threats'; U.S. carries out new strikes in Iran
items.summary: This comes as tensions in the Middle East escalated once again, with the U.S. military carrying out new strikes overnight in Iran.

items.title: Wall Street slips as crude oil jumps, Fed’s preferred inflation hits 3-year high
items.summary: In early trading, the S&P 500 slipped 0.1%, the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 328 points, and the Nasdaq Composite was 0.2% lower.

items.title: LIC looks to improve return from real estates, to explore option of separate subsidiary
items.summary: In the recent past, he said LIC has initiated a comprehensive review of its real estate portfolio to assess the returns and yields it generates, and to identify opportunities for further optimisation and improvement.

items.title: Asia Pacific real estate investment rises 19% in Q1; India emerges as key logistics, data centre growth market: Savills
items.summary: Industrial and logistics assets also continued to attract strong investor interest due to growing demand linked to AI-related manufacturing, semiconductor exports, data centre development and infrastructure expansion across markets including India, Japan, Taiwan and Malaysia.

items.title: Ousted BP chair clashed with company secretary
items.summary: Ben Mathews has taken time off work following upheaval at oil major

items.title: Arkade Developers Q4 loss at Rs 109.45 cr
items.summary: Total income rises to Rs 199.06 crore during the January-March period of 2025-26 fiscal from Rs 134.34 crore in the corresponding period a year-ago, according to a regulatory filing on Wednesday.

items.title: Merchant ships desert Strait of Hormuz amid renewed US strikes
items.summary: Commercial shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz nearly came to a halt on Thursday as fears of renewed military escalation between the US and Iran intensified. Ship-tracking data showed no commercial vessels crossing the key oil chokepoint Thursday morning after only six two-way crossings on Wednesday.

items.title: Reliance’s luxury retail arm narrows losses, designer labels lag
items.summary: Reliance Brands, Reliance Retail’s flagship premium and luxury fashion business, reported FY26 revenue of ₹3,494 crore, up 44.6% from a year earlier, while losses narrowed to ₹137 crore from ₹279 crore in FY25.

items.title: Crypto companies without EU licences face prosecution, French regulator warns
items.summary: Under the ​European Union's crypto rules, MiCA, crypto companies have until June ​30 to get a ⁠licence to ‌continue operating in ​the ​bloc. European regulators have ⁠already warned that companies without licences ​need to have "orderly wind-down plans" ​in place.

items.title: Vijay Kedia’s second pitch to turbocharge market after LTCG repeal: Scrap dividend taxation
items.summary: Making a case for rewarding patient, long-term capital market investors, Kedia in a social media post said that dividend income on listed equities should not be subjected to double taxation.

items.title: ECB says holding rates in April was a 'close call'
items.summary: The European Central Bank keeping rates on hold in April was a "close call", minutes released Thursday said, the latest sign that the bank is preparing to raise the cost of borrowing. Adding to evidence of a hawkish shift in ECB communications, Thursday's minutes said keeping rates where they were had been "a close call", conditional on the bank stressing it was keeping a close eye on prices.

items.title: In FY26, 97% of violative ads were on digital platforms, 4/5ths of them on Meta: Asci
items.summary: New data reveals digital platforms dominate problematic advertisements in the upcoming financial year. Meta alone is responsible for a significant portion of these campaigns. Offshore betting and realty sectors lead in violations. The advertising watchdog highlights a rise in exaggerated claims and misleading content, urging for stronger accountability and responsible digital advertising practices.

items.title: Key inflaton gauge worsens as Americans shell out more for gasoline
items.summary: Inflation jumped to 3.8% in April compared with a year ago, the Commerce Department said Thursday, up from 3.5% in March and the highest since May 2023. On a monthly basis, prices rose 0.4%, down from the 0.7% jump in March.

items.title: Muthoot Exim targets to mobilise 3 tonnes of gold in 2 years for recyling
items.summary: The company targets to open additional 50 outlets this fiscal especially in smaller cities, says its CEO Keyur Shah

items.title: US stocks today: US stocks hold near records even as crude oil prices rise again
items.summary: U.S. stocks hover near record highs despite economic pressures from the conflict with Iran. While oil prices rebound, strong corporate profits from companies like Dollar Tree and Snowflake are bolstering the market. Investors remain cautious amid conflicting reports on a potential U.S.-Iran deal.

items.title: Core inflation hit an annual rate of 3.3% in April, as expected, Fed’s preferred gauge shows
items.summary: The PCE price index for April was expected to show an annual inflation rate of 3.8% for all items and 3.3% for core.

items.title: Pharma stock declares 18% final dividend along with Q4 results 2026
items.summary: Lincoln Pharmaceuticals announced a final dividend of ₹1.80 per share for FY26, reporting a net profit of ₹11.63 crore in Q4FY26. The company aims for ₹1,000 crore revenue in three years, focusing on expanding its product portfolio and global presence.

items.title: US jobless claims rise to 215,000 but layoffs remain low despite Iran war uncertainty
items.summary: The persistently low number of claims suggests that most U.S. companies have not resorted to layoffs. But even if they're not cutting jobs, employers haven't been adding many either. Last year, companies, nonprofits and government agencies added fewer than 10,000 jobs a month, weakest hiring outside recession years since 2002.

items.title: The UK-India Free Trade Agreement
Gold tumbles to two-month low as inflation hedge status fades
items.summary: Spot gold prices were trading at their lowest level since late March.

items.title: India, Canada to avoid sensitive sectors in trade pact talks: Piyush Goyal
items.summary: The two countries are negotiating a Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA). The third round of negotiations is underway in Ottawa. The minister was in Canada for a three-day official visit.

items.title: Q4 Results Today Live: Ashok Leyland & JSW Holdings Q4 profit up, Graphite India reports Q4 loss, Bharat Dynamics, Alkem Lab & Anup Engineering profit down, Finolex Cables, Lemon Tree Hotels to announce Q4 results
items.summary: Q4 Results Highlights Today Live Updates: Stay tuned to businessline for the latest live updates on Q4 earnings.

items.title: US Stock Market Today | Dow Jones | Nasdaq Live: US stocks hold near records even as crude oil prices rise again
Ferrari CEO defends $640,000 price tag for its first fully electric car
items.summary: Ferrari CEO Benedetto Vigna on Thursday said the cost of the manufacturer's new Luce model was a fair price to pay for innovation.

items.title: Gold rate today: MCX gold prices tank over ₹2,000 to below ₹1,54000 as fresh US-Iran tensions raise inflation woes
items.summary: Gold prices on the MCX fell by 1.31% to ₹153,586 amid rising oil prices and inflation fears linked to US-Iran tensions. Global spot gold also declined, reflecting broader market weakness.

items.title: Young people out of work or training costing UK £125bn as report warns of 'perfect storm'
items.summary: A report warns the UK is "at risk of a lost generation" with number of 16 to 24-year-olds out of work, education or training set to rise to 1.25 million by 2031.

items.title: Oil prices jump 2% after Iran says it targeted U.S. airbase following fresh American strikes
items.summary: Oil prices rose on Thursday after fresh U.S. strikes in Iran renewed concerns over disruptions to commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: US stock market today: Dow Jones, Nasdaq slip as Middle East tensions intensify; Brent crude rebounds
items.summary: US stock futures pointed to a weak opening as tensions in West Asia raised crude oil prices, heightening inflation concerns. S&P 500 futures fell 0.3%, while Nasdaq and Dow futures also declined. Investors await PCE data for insights on future Fed policy amid ongoing geopolitical conflicts.

items.title: Why you should enter the FT’s new stock picking game
items.summary: Aside from the £1,000 cash prize, of course

items.title: This AI stock is surging after an ex-OpenAI employee's fund disclosed a stake. Here's why
items.summary: Dutch cloud provider Nebius popped in premarket trading after an ex-OpenAI employee's fund took a sizeable stake in the firm,

items.title: TCI plans to buy ships to expand high-margin coastal seaway business
items.summary: The Gurugram-based logistics and supply chain management company has planned a capex of ₹550-600 crore in FY27. Of this, ₹200-250 crore will go towards making final payments for acquiring two new ships from China.

items.title: Gold falls below Rs 1.54 lakh/10 grams, silver drops by Rs 4,600/kg as fresh US-Iran strikes raise worries
items.summary: Gold and silver prices on MCX plunged as US-Iran strikes boosted the dollar and oil, triggering inflation fears. Both metals hit multi-month lows as heightened geopolitical uncertainty and expectations of higher interest rates drove investors toward the dollar.

items.title: West Asia conflict may slow India’s growth, push up Inflation: SBI Chairman
items.summary: State Bank of India Chairman Challa Sreenivasulu Setty warns the West Asia conflict may slow global growth and increase inflation. India remains resilient due to strong domestic demand and public investment. However, spillover effects could impact India's GDP growth and inflation in FY2027. Policymakers and financial institutions must remain agile as global dynamics shift.

items.title: Quote of the day by Robert Wilson: "The only way one makes money in the market is when the market’s perception of a stock changes"
items.summary: Stock prices are driven by shifts in market perception, not just current performance. Investors make money when expectations change, moving from pessimism to optimism or recognizing overlooked value. Identifying these potential shifts early is crucial for significant investment gains as markets look ahead.

items.title: IMEC, Indo-Pacific routes key to reducing Hormuz dependence: EY
items.summary: India is urged to diversify its trade routes and develop new corridors like IMEC and the Indo-Pacific route. This aims to lessen dependence on the Strait of Hormuz amid West Asia tensions. The nation must prepare for economic shocks by building strategic reserves and shifting towards green energy.

items.title: Bharat Dynamics Q4 Results: Net profit tumbles 59% to Rs 113 crore; dividend announced
items.summary: Bharat Dynamics reported a 59% YoY net profit drop to Rs 113 crore for Q4 FY26, with revenue declining 73%. For the full fiscal year, profit fell 23% to Rs 420 crore. The board recommended a Rs 0.40 dividend.

items.title: CV industry showing resilience, not much impact of fuel price hike on demand: Ashok Leyland MD
items.summary: The Indian commercial vehicle sector shows remarkable strength. Fuel price increases from the West Asia conflict have not significantly impacted demand. Lower GST rates and the replacement of old vehicles are driving sales. Ashok Leyland expects continued strong performance in April and May. Operations in the UAE are normalizing after initial war-related disruptions.

items.title: RBI must let rupee depreciate; use liquidity tools, not rate hikes, to curtail inflation: Duvvuri Subbarao
items.summary: Former RBI Governor Duvvuri Subbarao has advised the RBI to allow some rupee depreciation to counter external pressures. He has suggested using liquidity measures instead of rate hikes if inflation risks rise. Subbarao has emphasised that monetary policy should be a last resort for exchange rate defence.

items.title: Indian small businesses record strongest growth since COVID in 2025: CPA Australia survey
items.summary: Indian small businesses saw their best growth in 2025 since the pandemic. Four out of five firms expanded, with strong expectations for 2026. Technology adoption and customer experience boosted growth. Rising costs and global uncertainty remain key concerns. Hiring increased, and digital payments and e-commerce revenue grew significantly.

items.title: Coal gasification scheme open to private sector, govt to provide 30-year coal linkage: MoS Dubey
items.summary: The Indian government is fully opening its coal gasification scheme to private sector players. Investors are assured of 30-year coal linkage support to encourage large-scale investments. The scheme welcomes independent participation or joint ventures. The government will support cost-effective technologies for Indian coal. Projects near coal production sites will reduce costs and pollution.

items.title: PMS industry sees strong April inflows; AUM rises above ₹42 lakh crore
items.summary: The total client base declined 1.7 per cent to about 2.12 lakh accounts in April

items.title: CVS to restore coverage of Zepbound, add Eli Lilly's obesity pill to drug plans
items.summary: CVS will add Zepbound coverage on Oct. 1, and start covering Lilly's newly approved Foundayo pill on June 1.

items.title: Ex-SJP partners allege wealth manager took clients without paying
items.summary: Former advisers preparing legal action over claims St James’s Place failed to pay compensation after taking their business

items.title: Ashok Leyland Q4 Results: Net profit rises 14% to Rs 1,291 crore, firm announces Rs 2.5 interim dividend
items.summary: Ashok Leyland reported a strong rise in fourth-quarter profit and revenue for FY26, driven by record commercial vehicle, export and light commercial vehicle volumes. The company also announced an interim dividend and highlighted growth across defence, electric mobility and aftermarket businesses.

items.title: Bajaj Finserv commits ₹1,500 cr-2,000 cr over 5 years to AI innovation startups to bridge private R&D funding gap
items.summary: The plan to invest in AI startups will focus on early-stage companies. The initiative, Finserv Intelligence, aims to enhance India's private sector R&D and will collaborate with IIT Bombay for advancements in AI and cybersecurity.

items.title: 4 things you can learn from legendary investor Warren Buffett’s lifestyle
items.summary: Warren Buffett’s simple lifestyle reflects his enduring financial philosophy focused on value, patience and discipline. From avoiding luxury spending to prioritising quality investments and protecting time, the Berkshire Hathaway veteran continues to inspire generations of investors with practical money and life lessons.

items.title: EU fines Temu €200m for allowing sale of illegal products
items.summary: The European Commission says the Chinese-owned online retailer failed to take account of risks from baby toys and faulty chargers sold on its platform.

items.title: Dr Reddy’s to ring NYSE Closing Bell to mark 25 years of listing
items.summary: Dr Reddy’s became the first pharmaceutical firm in Asia outside Japan to list on the NYSE in 2001

items.title: US Stock Market Today Live Updates: US stocks edge marginally lower; traders assess inflation data, Mideast developments
items.summary: US Stock Market Today Live Updates: Fresh tensions between US and Iran has dampened investor hopes of a swift resolution to the war. As a result, the US stock futures are pointing towards a tepid start for Wall Street today.

items.title: This multibagger renewable energy stock jumps 50% from March lows, hits five-month high. Do you own it?
items.summary: Suzlon Energy has seen a remarkable stock recovery, climbing 50% amid improved investor sentiment and rising renewable energy demand. The company reported strong quarterly earnings, and brokerages express confidence in its growth, maintaining 'Buy' ratings with target prices from ₹60 to ₹71.

items.title: Fuel price hikes may impact auto demand; too early to revise FY27 outlook: TMPV MD & CEO
items.summary: Fuel price hikes are a concern for the auto industry. Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles MD & CEO Shailesh Chandra says it is too early to assess the impact on demand. He remains optimistic about the company's growth prospects. EV demand is showing strong momentum. Capacity for EVs can be enhanced internally and with suppliers.

items.title: CNBC Daily Open: Oil falls on Iran peace hopes — then U.S. launches airstrikes
items.summary: Global markets rally as investors continue to price in Middle East de-escalation.

items.title: Tata AutoComp, Jahwa Electronics forge JV to manufacture heaters for EVs
items.summary: Tata AutoComp Systems has partnered with Korea's Jahwa Electronics. They will jointly manufacture advanced PTC heaters for electric and hybrid vehicles in India. This venture positions India as a key hub for Jahwa. It aims to supply both low-voltage and high-voltage PTC heaters. This move supports the growing investments in India's EV and hybrid vehicle markets.

items.title: Reliance warns of a volatile oil market in FY27, bets on gas and green chemicals for growth
items.summary: In the upcoming financial year FY26-27, Reliance Industries Limited is preparing for a potential slump in global oil demand, driven by high crude prices, economic slowdowns, and geopolitical unrest in the Middle East. However, the company is positioning itself to seize a long-term opportunity in the energy and materials sectors.

items.title: Indian semicon firm Netrasemi plans mass production of its first chip this year
items.summary: Indian AI chip maker Netrasemi is set to launch its A2000 chipset this year. The chip has successfully passed its initial testing phase. This system on chip will power smart cameras and edge AI devices. It enables real-time video analysis and on-device artificial intelligence. Netrasemi is collaborating with manufacturers for early sample evaluations.

items.title: Reliance operationalises clean energy giga complex; elevates national exchequer contribution to Rs 2,16,472 crore in FY26
items.summary: Reliance Industries has launched its clean energy operations. The company commissioned solar module manufacturing and began advanced work on a battery storage giga-factory. Reliance also significantly increased its contributions to the national exchequer. Jio Platforms accelerated its research and development, filing numerous new patents. The company is focused on enabling India's energy and digital future.

items.title: NHAI lines up 17 highway assets spanning 1,692 km for monetisation in FY27
items.summary: National Highways Authority of India plans to monetize 17 highway assets spanning over 1,600 km in FY 2026-27. These stretches are located across nine states. The monetisation will occur through Toll-Operate-Transfer and Infrastructure Investment Trust routes. This initiative aims to attract investment for highway development. It will unlock capital for new construction projects.

items.title: Govt readies contingency plans to counter El Nino threat to kharif season: Shivraj Singh Chouhan
items.summary: The Indian government is ready for El Nino's impact on the upcoming kharif crop. Contingency plans are being made for affected districts. The focus is on integrated farming and achieving self-reliance in pulses and oilseeds. Efforts are underway to ensure seed availability and explore alternative crops. This proactive approach aims to secure agricultural production despite potential weather challenges.

items.title: Reliance secures record Japan financing after S&P upgrade to A-
items.summary: Reliance Industries Limited achieved a major milestone by securing the largest Samurai loan ever for an Indian company. It also completed three unique global financing deals in FY 2025-26. These achievements followed a significant credit rating upgrade by S&P Global Ratings. The company successfully tapped diverse international funding sources, demonstrating its financial strength and global reach.

items.title: Reliance Jio IPO soon? Mukesh Ambani to provide update on $4 bn public issue at upcoming AGM, says report
items.summary: Mukesh Ambani is expected to share an update on Jio Platforms’ anticipated IPO at Reliance Industries’ June 19 AGM. Reports suggest Reliance is shifting towards a fresh issue structure to protect retail investors and strengthen Jio’s long-term growth and transparency roadmap.

items.title: Mukesh Ambani-led Reliance Industries' contribution to national exchequer rises to Rs 2.16 lakh crore
items.summary: Reliance Industries’ contribution to the national exchequer rose nearly 3% to Rs 2.16 lakh crore in FY26. The company said government payments formed the largest share of value created, while revenue, EBITDA and annual profit recorded strong year-on-year growth.

items.title: Rare earth, gas supply, aircraft parts: What’s inside the new IIP calculus as India revises base year
items.summary: India is updating its Index of Industrial Production. The new series will use 2022-23 as the base year, replacing the old 2011-12 base. This revision will include new items and sectors, offering a more accurate picture of industrial output. The updated index aims to capture emerging industries and technological progress.

items.title: Ousted BP chairman hits back at 'lies' about his behaviour
items.summary: Albert Manifold said no-one should be "allowed to hide behind anonymity" when commenting on his time at BP.

items.title: ET Alpha Wealth Summit: Navigating the great wealth shift in an uncertain market cycle
items.summary: The ET Alpha Summit in Mumbai brings together leading market voices to explore how investors should navigate expensive markets amid geopolitical uncertainty, inflation and volatility. The sessions focus on Saurabh Mukherjea’s take on the disappearance of cheap stocks and the need to rethink valuation discipline, alongside Samir Arora’s view on where India’s next wave of wealth creation will emerge through structural shifts and evolving market leadership.

items.title: SC seeks Centre, ED responses on plea alleging Rs 14,000 crore diversion in Noida, Yamuna Expressway housing projects
items.summary: The Supreme Court has issued notices to the Centre, Enforcement Directorate, RBI, Uttar Pradesh authorities, and several real estate firms. This follows a plea alleging diversion of thousands of crores collected from homebuyers in Noida and Yamuna Expressway projects. Advocate Prashant Bhushan highlighted a pattern of developers diverting funds and pushing projects into insolvency.

items.title: Ashok Leyland Q4 Results: Firm posts highest-ever quarterly PAT at ₹1,405 crore; ₹2.50/share dividend declared
items.summary: The Board of Directors of Ashok Leyland, at their meeting held today, have declared a second interim dividend of ₹2.50 per equity share of ₹1/- each for the financial year ended March 31, 2026.

items.title: P&G Hygiene Q4 Results: Net profit remains flat at ₹156 crore; declares ₹60/share final dividend
items.summary: In Q4FY26, Procter & Gamble Hygiene and Health Care's revenue fell 5% to ₹941.32 crore due to softer demand. However, FY26 saw revenue rise to ₹4,290.42 crore, with net profit increasing to ₹856.50 crore. Leadership changes were also announced.

items.title: Mukesh Ambani draws zero salary again: India's richest man forgoes paycheck for 6th straight year
items.summary: Reliance Industries Chairman Mukesh Ambani did not take any salary in FY26, continuing a six-year trend. Reliance reported strong annual revenue growth despite profit decline in Q4, while Ambani’s children and senior executives received commissions and remuneration.

items.title: Iraq faces summer blackouts
items.summary: Iran war has exposed oil-rich nation’s dependence on Iranian gas for electricity as temperatures exceed 45C

items.title: IPO investors brace for 70 lock-in expiries worth $35 billion in three months. Do you own any?
items.summary: According to Nuvama Alternative & Quantitative Research, the Indian stock market is set to witness a significant wave of lock-in expiries between May 26 and August 31, as shares of 70 recently listed companies worth nearly $35 billion become eligible for trading.

items.title: IBC: Creditors realised Rs 4 lakh cr; cases involving Rs 14 lakh cr settled in pre-admission stage
items.summary: India's Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC) has facilitated over Rs 4 lakh crore in creditor realisations from resolved cases. One hundred twenty new items are added, such as CCTV cameras and vaccines. Sixty-four items are removed. This update provides a more comprehensive view of industrial activity.

items.title: American Air Sees Robust Demand Even as Consumer Sentiment Falls
items.summary: American Airlines Group Inc. said it continues to see strong demand for travel, even as surging jet fuel drives up ticket prices and consumer confidence in the US edges down.

items.title: DGGI set to fire up GST recovery drive against gaming firms after Supreme Court's backing
items.summary: The Directorate General of GST Intelligence will now aggressively pursue tax recovery from online gaming companies after the Supreme Court upheld a 28% GST levy on the full bet value. This ruling validates tax evasion allegations of approximately ₹1 lakh crore against around 80 companies, paving the way for significant recovery proceedings.

items.title: Piyush Goyal meets Canadian pension funds, investors in push for $50 bn trade
items.summary: India's Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal is in Canada to forge new partnerships. He met with top Canadian pension funds and investors. Discussions focused on clean energy, technology, AI, and manufacturing. Canada announced a trade mission to India in November. Both nations aim to increase bilateral trade to $50 billion by 2030 and finalize an economic agreement.

items.title: US trade negotiators to visit India from June 1-4 for interim trade deal talks

items.title: Too hot to handle: Heatwave in North India slows textile, rice supply chains
items.summary: Northern India's blistering heatwaves are wreaking havoc on vital industries, significantly hampering the trade of Basmati rice and textile production in the Delhi-NCR region. Workers face unbearable temperatures, resulting in increased absenteeism and interruptions in manufacturing processes. Additionally, erratic power supply complicates timely export shipments.

A more stable and profitable environment is needed to attract capital.

The agriculture ministry confirms stable and resilient wheat production.

Measures like tax relief, import curbs can shield India from West Asia crisis: Report
items.summary: India must adopt tax moderation and targeted import curbs to boost exports and shield against the West Asia crisis. A report suggests restricting non-essential imports to protect domestic industries and foreign exchange. This strategy aims for macroeconomic fortification, moving beyond subsidies. Reducing non-essential imports is crucial for national security and economic resilience.

items.title: Supreme Court backs Tamil Nadu, Karnataka online gaming laws on online rummy, poker with stakes
items.summary: The Supreme Court has affirmed states' authority to control and prohibit online gaming involving betting. This decision overturns earlier High Court rulings that had struck down state laws restricting online rummy and poker with stakes. The Court stated that even games of skill can be regulated if they impact public well-being.

items.title: West Asia crisis may soon make everyday goods costlier in India
items.summary: India faces a looming price hike on everyday goods as global commodity costs surge due to the West Asia conflict. Manufacturers are absorbing rising input expenses, from metals to chemicals, with little price increase so far. This pressure is expected to soon translate into higher consumer prices, impacting household budgets.

This decline impacts Indian prices, which have already fallen. Non-basmati rice exports showed a small increase. Delays in transit due to the ongoing conflict are affecting new deals.
